stories.body: U.S. Central Command said American troops shot down four Iranian drones aimed at Strait of Hormuz shipping lanes before destroying Iranian coastal radar and surveillance sites at Goruk and Qeshm Island, part of a wider conflict that began with joint U.S.-Israeli strikes in February; President Donald Trump told NBC that Iran retains roughly 21% of its missile arsenal even as Tehran ties any regional cease-fire to an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon and continued support for Hezbollah keeps violence flaring from Lebanon to Gaza despite U.S.-brokered pauses.
stories.headline: U.S. forces strike Iranian coastal radar sites after intercepting drones near Hormuz

stories.body: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Lebanon’s Al Mayadeen that the war with the United States will not end without an Israeli retreat from southern Lebanon, reiterating that Tehran’s support for Hezbollah and leverage over the Strait of Hormuz are central to any interim deal even as Hezbollah rejects a U.S.-brokered truce, carries out fresh attacks near Beaufort Castle, and Israel keeps striking Lebanese towns amid concerns from Washington about escalation.
stories.headline: Iran links cease-fire talks to Israeli pullout as Hezbollah fights on

stories.body: A new Amnesty International report based on 247 interviews and satellite analysis alleges Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces committed murder, rape, enslavement, torture, persecution of non-Arab civilians, and ethnic cleansing while besieging and ultimately capturing El Fasher between mid-2024 and late 2025, orphaning children, displacing hundreds of thousands, and destroying towns such as Abu Zerega in a campaign the organization says bears the hallmarks of genocide identified by a U.N. fact-finding mission.
stories.headline: Amnesty International accuses Sudan’s RSF of crimes against humanity in El Fasher

stories.body: NASA said five astronauts briefly sheltered inside their SpaceX Crew Dragon when an air leak aboard Russia’s Zvezda module doubled to about two pounds per day, prompting disagreement with Roscosmos over using a saw to reach the suspected crack; after Russia paused repairs, mission control allowed the crew to re-enter the station, noting that evacuation procedures—never fully executed in the ISS’s 27-year history—were triggered as a precaution amid ongoing debates about extending the station’s life to 2032.
stories.headline: NASA rescinds ISS evacuation order after reviewing Russian air leak

stories.body: Côte d’Ivoire’s government said 59 people have died since May as relentless coastal rains inundate neighborhoods, with Ghana reporting 13 deaths and hundreds of rescues, Benin, Togo, and Nigeria facing submerged infrastructure, and residents citing climate change, rapid urbanization, and waste-choked drainage systems as Accra’s president John Mahama warns of record rainfall and authorities brace for even heavier downpours across the region.
stories.headline: West Africa reels from floods that kill 59 in Côte d’Ivoire

stories.body: Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko reported fires in the Podil district, a burning nine-story apartment block, and cars ignited by debris in Obolon as Russian forces launched a heavy strike on the Ukrainian capital, days after Moscow warned of systematic attacks on military-linked targets in Kyiv following a deadly blast in occupied Luhansk that Ukraine denies carrying out, while President Volodymyr Zelenskiy urged residents to heed air-raid alerts against a possible massive assault.
stories.headline: Kyiv endures massive missile and drone barrage amid Russian threats

stories.body: In a 52–47 party-line vote, Senate Republicans advanced a bill granting the Department of Homeland Security $70 billion for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol operations over the next three years, rejecting Democratic and some Republican attempts to eliminate President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, which critics call a slush fund for allies even as acting Attorney General nominee Todd Blanche pledged not to use it and GOP senators sparred over amendments amid election-year pressures.
stories.headline: Senate approves $70 billion DHS enforcement boost while leaving Trump’s ally compensation fund untouched

stories.body: Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang ruled that dozens of Missouri abortion limits, including mandatory dual clinic visits separated by 72 hours and in-person dosing requirements for medication abortion, violate a 2024 constitutional amendment restoring access up to fetal viability, paving the way for Planned Parenthood to prescribe abortion pills in-state for the first time since 2018 even as the state keeps an in-person exam to confirm gestational age and the attorney general readies an appeal.
stories.headline: Missouri judge strikes down multiple abortion restrictions under voter-approved amendment

stories.body: Authorities said erratic winds drove the Aspen Acres wildfire to 115 square miles southwest of Denver with zero containment, destroying over 160 structures, prompting evacuations of Colorado City, Beulah, Rye, and San Isabel, and drawing Colorado National Guard troops to secure checkpoints while crews also battled the Snyder, Cottonwood, and Babylon fires elsewhere in the region amid a dry winter and dozens of large blazes across the western United States.
stories.headline: Colorado’s Aspen Acres fire destroys more than 160 structures and forces mass evacuations

stories.body: National Court judge Santiago Pedraz charged Guardia Civil director Mercedes González and operational chief Manuel Llamas with continued malfeasance and obstruction after prosecutors alleged they used disciplinary probes to intimidate the UCO anti-corruption unit investigating cases tied to the PSOE, including probes involving the Sánchez family, even as the government says it still trusts the officials pending their July 16 testimony.
stories.headline: Spain’s Guardia Civil director and operations chief charged with intimidating elite anti-corruption unit

stories.body: Spain’s tax authority told National Court judge José Luis Calama it has launched income, wealth, VAT, and corporate tax inspections covering 2021–2024 for former prime minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero, his wife, daughters, and businessman Julio Martínez, key figures in the Plus Ultra lobbying probe, and asked whether to suspend them due to potential prejudice to the criminal case after authorities seized €1.3 million in jewelry at Zapatero’s office and alleged the group facilitated illicit payments for the airline.
stories.headline: Tax agency opens inspections of Zapatero family amid Plus Ultra corruption case

stories.body: Following coalition deals in Extremadura, Aragón, Castilla y León, and now Andalusia, the PP and Vox have signed 184 pages of programmatic commitments combining progressive cuts to regional income, inheritance, and housing taxes with Vox-backed “national priority” rules restricting access to public aid and services based on territorial ties, signaling that the two parties expect to negotiate similar arrangements nationally despite concerns about moderation and Vox’s influence in multiple autonomous governments.
stories.headline: PP-Vox regional pacts entrench tax cuts and hard-line migration policies

stories.body: Catalan authorities said the Bisbal d’Empordà wildfire has entered the Gavarres range, scorched more than 1,000 hectares, forced multiple evacuations and confinements, and led President Salvador Illa to seek potential deployment of the military emergency unit as firefighters race to contain the right flank ahead of forecast heat waves while investigators detained a contractor accused of sparking the blaze with forbidden metal grinding work during high-risk conditions.
stories.headline: Gavarres wildfire burns over 1,000 hectares and prompts request for military help

stories.body: After a 15-year-old was fatally shot in Barcelona’s Parc de la Pegaso, Mossos d'Esquadra director Josep Lluís Trapero called the killing an intolerable red line, urged urgent Penal Code reforms to mandate prison for illegal firearm possession, and announced plans to add roughly 1,500 officers annually while expanding intelligence-led policing to curb youth gang violence and rising gun crime in Catalonia.
stories.headline: Teen shot dead in Sant Andreu spurs calls for tougher gun laws

stories.body: Barcelona’s hosting of the Tour de France Grand Départ on 2, 4, and 5 July is closing major corridors across six districts, rerouting dozens of bus and tram lines, restricting parking along the 19.6-kilometer team time-trial circuit from the Fòrum to Montjuïc, and prompting authorities to advise metro use while installing special pedestrian crossings, bike parking, and direct traffic updates as an estimated 850,000 visitors arrive for the stages and team presentations.
stories.headline: Tour de France Grand Départ triggers sweeping road closures and transit changes

stories.body: The Barcelona City Council finalized the reparceling plan for the 44-hectare Sector Prim near La Sagrera, clearing the way to demolish remaining industrial warehouses by 2027 and begin building 3,359 homes—2,041 of them municipally owned, including 1,637 protected units and 200 supportive apartments—along with 21,600 square meters of schools, health, cultural, and sports facilities anchored around the historic Can Riera farmhouse and a new public square linking Sant Martí and Sant Andreu districts.
stories.headline: Barcelona approves Sector Prim redevelopment with more than 2,000 public homes
